summary: Large symbol-renaming PR with no diff captured in the slice. The PR body says it replaced generic unnamed-symbol prefixes like `lbl_` and `func_` with module-based prefixes, named Fighter state functions from state IDs, and standardized fighter-related names around HAL's two-character fighter codes discovered from game data strings. It was merged quickly without recorded review comments in this slice.
what_changed: Renamed many symbols across the project, but the slice contains no changed-file list or diff, so exact files and symbol names are not available here.
Changed unnamed-symbol convention from generic `lbl_` or `func_` prefixes to prefixes based on the owning module.
Named Fighter state functions based on Fighter state IDs, with the PR body crediting the `peppi` action_state enum as a reference.
Adopted HAL's two-character fighter abbreviations for fighter naming, extracted from `PlXX.dat`-style strings in the built `main.elf`.

Documented a table mapping English fighter names to short HAL-style codes, including examples such as Common `Co`, Mario `Mr`, Captain Falcon `Ca`, Fox `Fx`, Link `Lk`, Kirby `Kb`, Donkey Kong `Dk`, Bowser `Kp` for Koopa, Marth `Ms` for Mars, Jigglypuff `Pr` for Purin, Young Link `Cl` for Child Link, Roy `Fe` for Fire Emblem, Master Hand `Mh`, and Crazy Hand `Ch`.
Pointed readers to an external gist for renames that were not simple module-prefix changes and to `melee-diff-symbols` for the full symbol diff.
smart_moves: Used module-based prefixes for still-unnamed symbols, making placeholder names more searchable and informative than `lbl_` or `func_`.
Grounded Fighter state-function names in state IDs rather than ad hoc names, improving consistency and making state lookup easier.
Reused HAL's own two-character fighter naming scheme instead of inventing a project-specific abbreviation scheme.
Provided a reproducible command pipeline using `strings -d build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.elf | grep 'Pl[A-Z][a-z]\+\.dat' | sed 's/Pl\(..\)\.dat/\1/'` to recover the fighter codes from the built ELF.
Separated ordinary module-prefix renames from nontrivial renames by linking an external gist, helping reviewers and future contributors audit the meaningful naming changes.
decomp_lessons: For mass symbol renames, prefer names that encode module ownership so unknown functions and labels remain useful before they are fully identified.
When naming state-machine functions, use authoritative numeric state IDs or enums where available to avoid inconsistent or speculative names.
Game asset filenames and embedded strings can reveal developer naming conventions; those conventions are valuable for decomp symbol naming.
Mass rename PRs should explain the naming rule and provide tooling or references for reproducing the mapping, because the raw diff may be too large to review manually.
This slice has no diff or changed-file data, so downstream users should consult the linked PR, gist, or `melee-diff-symbols` before relying on exact symbol-level details.
naming_conventions: Unnamed symbols should be prefixed with their module instead of generic `lbl_` or `func_`.
Fighter state functions should be named from their Fighter state ID.
Fighter-related symbols should use HAL's two-character fighter abbreviations where applicable.

HAL fighter code examples recorded in the PR body include `Co`, `Mr`, `Ca`, `Fx`, `Lk`, `Kb`, `Dk`, `Sk`, `Ns`, `Pe`, `Pp`, `Nn`, `Pk`, `Ss`, `Ys`, `Kp`, `Ms`, `Zd`, `Pr`, `Lg`, `Mt`, `Cl`, `Dr`, `Fc`, `Pc`, `Gw`, `Gn`, `Fe`, `Bo`, `Gl`, `Gk`, `Sb`, `Mh`, and `Ch`.

Some HAL abbreviations reflect Japanese/internal names rather than English display names, e.g. Bowser as `Kp`/Koopa, Marth as `Ms`/Mars, Jigglypuff as `Pr`/Purin, Young Link as `Cl`/Child Link, and Roy as `Fe`/Fire Emblem.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `strings -d build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.elf` plus filtering for `Pl[A-Z][a-z]+.dat` asset names to infer HAL two-character fighter codes.
Use `melee-diff-symbols` for reviewing or generating the complete symbol-rename diff when the normal PR diff is too large or absent from the dump.
External enum references such as peppi's `action_state.rs` can help map Fighter state IDs to meaningful state-function names.
follow_up_queries: Find the actual symbol diff for PR #780 using the linked gist or `melee-diff-symbols`.
Search current codebase for remaining generic `lbl_` or `func_` symbols that could be converted to module-prefixed placeholders.
Search Fighter state-function names for consistency with state IDs and HAL two-character fighter codes.
Audit whether later PRs refined or reverted any abbreviations introduced by this mass rename.
